commitment_text: I would like you to visit Member States regularly. I encourage you to go local, visit places and meet Europeans from across our Union, including in cities, rural and sparsely populated areas. Our network of Representations will support you in this. You should meet with representatives from regional or local areas, notably relying on our network of EU Local Councillors, and to give more visibility to EU projects making a difference to people’s lives on the ground. When engaging with our international partners you will also be supported by our network of EU delegations around the world. I expect you to actively communicate on the Commission’s actions and decisions and explain the benefits and opportunities stemming from our work. You should also help tackle disinformation, notably by providing clear and accurate information at all times. Third, this Commission will start a new era of dialogue with citizens and stakeholders. You should organise a first edition of the annual Youth Policy Dialogues within the first 100 days so that young people can be heard and can help shape your work. This will be part of our work in embedding citizen participation in our work. We will build on the Conference on the Future of Europe to instil a true and lasting culture of participative democracy. We will choose policy areas and proposals where recommendations from a European Citizens’ Panel would have the greatest value and follow up on their proposals.

commitment_text: You will ensure that the rules on transparency and meetings with interest representatives are fully respected, including for your staff and services. We will strengthen our transparency system by extending the requirements of the Transparency Register to all managers. Fifth, we will ensure that the Commission becomes more representative of the people we serve. We have made a lot of progress in terms of gender balance across management but there is clearly more to be done. Reflecting the EU’s strength in diversity, your Cabinet should ensure gender and geographical balance. While the Berlaymont will remain your primary headquarters, you will also have an office within your Directorate-General and be expected to be present on a regular basis to work closely with your staff and services. We will collectively ensure that the Commission is an inclusive, respectful and safe workplace. Making Europe simpler and faster The success of this new Commission will be measured against our ability to meet the targets and objectives we set, notably as part of the European Green Deal. You will be responsible for the delivery of the policy objectives and targets within your portfolio. To achieve this, you should make full use of all instruments for implementation and enforcement, including infringement proceedings. You will ensure that existing rules are fit-for-purpose and focus on reducing administrative burdens and simplifying legislation. You must contribute to reducing reporting obligations by at least 25% – and for SMEs at least 35%. You should leverage the power of digital tools to deliver better and faster solutions. We must listen to all companies and stakeholders who work on a daily basis to comply with EU legislation. You will organise at least two Implementation Dialogues per year with stakeholders to align implementation with realities on the ground. You will prepare an Annual Progress Report on Enforcement and Implementation for your respective Parliamentary committees and Council formations. You will also work to stress test the EU acquis and table proposals to eliminate any overlaps and contradictions and be fully digitally compatible, while maintaining high standards. New legislation must ensure that our rules are simpler, more accessible to citizens and more targeted. You will ensure the principles of proportionality, subsidiarity and Better Regulation are respected, including through wide consultations, impact assessments, a review by the independent Regulatory Scrutiny Board and a new SME and competitiveness check. Proposals must be evidence-based and the Joint Research Centre, our internal scientific service, can support you in that work.

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for Tech Sovereignty, Security and Democracy
commitment_text: You will also be responsible for the Digital and Frontier Technologies portfolio. Tech Sovereignty, Security and Democracy Security is fundamental for people’s safety and peace of mind, trust in our economies, societies and democracies. Our competitiveness objectives – and our wider ambitions in the years ahead - rely on combatting increasingly complex security threats. These range from the war on our borders in Ukraine and the need for Europe to do more to defend itself, to hybrid and cyber threats, attacks on critical infrastructure, foreign information manipulation and interference, as well as the weaponisation of people and all types of policies. Europe’s security also depends on having strong external borders. Your task will be to guide the work to strengthen operational capabilities for border management and respond to threats at the EU’s external borders, working closely with Member States. It will also be crucial to continue working with a fair and firm approach on migration, implementing the Pact on Asylum and Migration working with Member States and EU agencies to turn legal commitments into action and completing the work where necessary. You should also help guide the work in strengthening our area of freedom, security and justice. I would also like you to guide the work on strengthening our internal security, supporting a tougher approach on crime and looking at organised crime and the high-risk criminal networks which damage lives, businesses and entire economies. We must sharpen our tools, ensure cooperation of law enforcement agencies and tackle the most harmful issues, such as the rise in drug trafficking. You should also pay close attention to strengthening the resilience and the functioning our democracy, notably through the new European Democracy Shield and work to counter harmful disinformation which can cause rifts in society and weaken our democracy. The strength of our democracy will depend on the respect for our fundamental rights, values and the rule of law. You will help guide the work to strengthen our approach to the rule of law in areas such as judicial independence, anti-corruption, media freedom and other institutional checks and balances, while ensuring that we look at issues in the Single Market which affect the ability of companies to grow and do business across borders. We must always ensure that respect for the rule of law is imperative for EU funds.

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for Tech Sovereignty, Security and Democracy
commitment_text: I would like you to help guide the work in ensuring that this not only strengthens our security but also drives our defence industrial competitiveness. Building a single market for defence goods and services, ensuring innovation and private and public investment will be key in this regard. I would like you to bring all of our work together to ensure high standards of cybersecurity. You will look at how digital technologies and Artificial Intelligence offer significant opportunities to enhance law enforcement capabilities and address these evolving threats effectively. This will be part of work to ensure that we strengthen our digital competitiveness. You will also work with the Executive Vice-President for Prosperity and Industrial Strategy to boost productivity with digital tech diffusion to help transform industries and develop new services and business models. Digital and frontier technologies This digital transformation has proven to be a game-changer for society’s ability to tackle major challenges, from productivity and growth, to fighting climate change or improving our public services. Digital technologies are at the heart of our progress and ambitions but we must address new challenges for our democracies, economies, societies. Your task is to lead Europe’s efforts in shaping a competitive, resilient and inclusive digital future. We must exploit our strengths in order to maintain or attain leadership in strategic technologies, to establish essential assets for technological sovereignty and resilience, and to foster commercialisation of deep tech innovation. • You will oversee our path towards reaching Europe's 2030 Digital Decade targets. I would like you to lead the review of the implementation strategy and digital targets in 2026, looking at where updates are needed in light of technological developments, cybersecurity concerns and our wider productivity and sustainability goals. • You will lead work on boosting Artificial Intelligence innovation, making it safer and more trustworthy. In the first 100 days, you will ensure access to tailored supercomputing capacity for AI start-ups and industry through the AI Factories initiative. You will develop, with Member States, industry and civil society, an Apply AI Strategy to boost new industrial uses of AI and improve delivery of public services. You will help set up a European AI Research Council. • You will intensify our efforts and investments concerning the next wave of frontier technologies, in particular supercomputing, semiconductors, the Internet of Things, genomics, quantum computing, space tech and beyond. • As part of boosting high performance computing and quantum, you will develop the proposal in the Draghi Report for an EU Cloud and AI Development Act to

You should incentivise and encourage investments in digital infrastructure, taking into account responses to the Commission’s White Paper of February 2024. • You will contribute to strengthening cybersecurity to protect our industries, citizens and public administrations against internal and external threats, notably by improving the adoption process of European cybersecurity certification schemes. You will contribute to an action plan on cybersecurity of hospitals and healthcare providers. • You will ensure the timely and predictable implementation of the European Digital Rulebook to boost productivity and protect our security and democracy. You will prioritise guidance and standardisation that facilitates compliance and assess, during the first year of the mandate, whether the expanded digital acquis adequately reflects the needs and constraints of SMEs and small midcaps. • You will ensure the Commission takes rapid and effective enforcement actions under the Digital Services Act and the Digital Markets Act whenever necessary. commitment_text: You will contribute to the EU-wide enquiry on the impacts of social media and the action plan against cyberbullying. • I want you to ensure an approach to the media which recognises its unique place in our democracies and culture, while also supporting its commercial development. You will consider the need to further improve the copyright framework to address new challenges raised by market and technology developments. • You will work to promote EU digital norms and standards internationally and to ensure a leading role for the EU in global digital governance, in particular for AI and cybersecurity. • You will ensure that the Commission becomes more resilient to cybersecurity threats, notably by reinforcing the security culture, protecting people and sensitive information, and ensuring the Commission is able to act during a crisis. • I want you to accelerate the digitalisation of the Commission, notably by exploring how processes can be further automated, and how AI can improve delivery and inform policymaking in a safe and secure way. You will focus on the continuous improvement of staff's digital skills and the full use of data across the organisation. • You will also work with the Commissioner for International Partnerships to support Global Gateway projects in partner countries, such as trustworthy digital communications networks. You will work under my guidance on all of the above issues. The Directorate-General for Communications Networks, Content and Technology and the Directorate-General for Digital Services will support you in your work on the digital and frontier technologies portfolio. Way forward The actions listed in this letter will shape your work. However, we will need to complete and adapt as we go along and I count on your ideas and expertise in this regard. In addition to College meetings and our continuous discussions, I will invite you every six months for a structured reporting meeting to discuss progress and challenges in delivering on your mission. Reflecting the evolving nature of political priorities, the Commission must remain agile in allocating staff where most needed. I expect you to play a collegial role in this, by working with your services to identify efficiencies and contributing to corporate redeployment efforts across the Commission.

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for Clean, Just and Competitive Transition
commitment_text: I would like you to visit Member States regularly. I encourage you to go local, visit places and meet Europeans from across our Union, including in cities, rural and sparsely populated areas. Our network of Representations will support you in this. You should meet with representatives from regional or local areas, notably relying on our network of EU Local Councillors, and to give more visibility to EU projects making a difference to people’s lives on the ground. When engaging with our international partners you will also be supported by our network of EU delegations around the world. I expect you to actively communicate on the Commission’s actions and decisions and explain the benefits and opportunities stemming from our work. You should also help tackle disinformation, notably by providing clear and accurate information at all times. Third, this Commission will start a new era of dialogue with citizens and stakeholders. You should organise a first edition of the annual Youth Policy Dialogues within the first 100 days so that young people can be heard and can help shape your work. This will be part of our work in embedding citizen participation in our work. We will build on the Conference on the Future of Europe to instil a true and lasting culture of participative democracy. We will choose policy areas and proposals where recommendations from a European Citizens’ Panel would have the greatest value and follow up on their proposals. Fourth, every Member of College must show a true European commitment beyond doubt and be fully independent in their action. I expect full adherence to all applicable integrity, impartiality and ethical rules from all, starting with the Code of Conduct. You will ensure that the rules on transparency and meetings with interest representatives are fully respected, including for your staff and services. We will strengthen our transparency system by extending the requirements of the Transparency Register to all managers.

commitment_text: you will also have an office within your Directorate-General and be expected to be present on a regular basis to work closely with your staff and services. We will collectively ensure that the Commission is an inclusive, respectful and safe workplace. Making Europe simpler and faster The success of this new Commission will be measured against our ability to meet the targets and objectives we set, notably as part of the European Green Deal. You will be responsible for the delivery of the policy objectives and targets within your portfolio. To achieve this, you should make full use of all instruments for implementation and enforcement, including infringement proceedings. You will ensure that existing rules are fit-for-purpose and focus on reducing administrative burdens and simplifying legislation. You must contribute to reducing reporting obligations by at least 25% – and for SMEs at least 35%. You should leverage the power of digital tools to deliver better and faster solutions. We must listen to all companies and stakeholders who work on a daily basis to comply with EU legislation. You will organise at least two Implementation Dialogues per year with stakeholders to align implementation with realities on the ground. You will prepare an Annual Progress Report on Enforcement and Implementation for your respective Parliamentary committees and Council formations. You will also work to stress test the EU acquis and table proposals to eliminate any overlaps and contradictions and be fully digitally compatible, while maintaining high standards. New legislation must ensure that our rules are simpler, more accessible to citizens and more targeted. You will ensure the principles of proportionality, subsidiarity and Better Regulation are respected, including through wide consultations, impact assessments, a review by the independent Regulatory Scrutiny Board and a new SME and competitiveness check. Proposals must be evidence-based and the Joint Research Centre, our internal scientific service, can support you in that work.

commitment_text: I would like you to guide the work to ensure that we stay the course on our goals set out in the European Green Deal. You will coordinate the work, together with the Executive Vice- President for Prosperity and Industrial Strategy and working with the Commissioner for Climate, Net Zero and Clean Growth, on the Clean Industrial Deal. The aim will be to unlock investment, create lead markets for clean tech and put in place conditions for companies to grow and compete. You will guide the work on the implementation of the existing legal framework to help reach our 2030 targets in the simplest, fairest way and in dialogue with all stakeholders. Once the 90% emission-reduction target for 2040 is enshrined in the European Climate Law, we will need to work closely with all stakeholders to prepare a new architecture beyond 2030 to enable us to reach net-zero by mid-century. As the Draghi report highlighted, one of the biggest breaks on our competitiveness and our industries’ ability to grow is high energy prices. I would like you to guide the work to bring down energy prices and rid ourselves of our dependencies on fossil fuels, working closely with the Commissioner for Energy and Housing. You will also work together to invest in clean energy infrastructure and to address energy poverty across Europe as part of addressing the housing crisis. Your role will be essential to ensure that Europe’s transition is for the benefit of people and their jobs, helping to boost our economy and industry and ensuring no one is left behind. In this spirit, you will guide work to ensure that taxation measures support a just transition and decarbonisation, working closely with the Commissioner for Climate, Net Zero and Clean Growth. You will work with other Members of College to steer our investment and

commitment_text: You will be responsible for the Competition portfolio. A clean, just and competitive transition Europe’s future competitiveness depends on getting decarbonisation right and ensuring a just transition. This reflects the analysis of the Draghi report which called for a decarbonised and circular economy and is in line with the driving purpose of the European Green Deal, designed as our growth strategy and central to our new industrial policy. To support this, Europe needs a new approach to competition policy – one that is more supportive of companies scaling up in global markets, allows European businesses and consumers to reap all the benefits of effective competition and is better geared to our common goals, including decarbonisation and a just transition. Our aim must be to ensure a level playing field, ensure that business has the incentive to invest, innovate and grow, and help protect consumers from rising prices and lowering quality of goods and services. portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for Clean, Just and Competitive Transition
commitment_text: you will modernise the EU's competition policy to ensure it supports European companies to innovate, compete and lead world-wide and contributes to our wider objectives on competitiveness and sustainability, social fairness and security. This will be an integral part of the work to make this an Investment Commission. • As part of the Clean Industrial Deal, you should develop a new State aid framework to accelerate the roll-out of renewable energy, to deploy industrial decarbonisation and to ensure sufficient manufacturing capacity of clean tech. This should build on the experience of the Temporary Crisis and Transition Framework and preserve cohesion objectives. Strong State aid control continues to play a key role to address market failures while avoiding inefficiencies in public spending. • Your work to modernise competition policy will include a review of the Horizontal Merger Control Guidelines. This should give adequate weight to the European economy’s more acute needs in respect of resilience, efficiency and innovation, the time horizons and investment intensity of competition in certain strategic sectors, and the changed defence and security environment. • You should preserve the level playing field while pursuing further simplification of State Aid, prioritising work on the most distortive aids and drawing the lessons from the temporary crisis and transition frameworks regarding decarbonisation of energy systems and industries. • You will focus on the particular challenges facing SMEs and small midcaps, notably to address risks of killer acquisitions from foreign companies seeking to eliminate them as a possible source of future competition. • You will support the implementation of the future European Competitiveness Fund to ensure coherence between it and State Aid policy, and the work with Member States for swift proposals for Important Projects of Common Interest (IPCEIs) for the most strategic sectors and technologies that can quickly access finance and get off the ground. • I would like you to lead the work, in close cooperation with Member States, to make State Aid review of IPCEIs simpler and faster and to examine the proposal in the Draghi Report to expand IPCEIs for innovation in strategic sectors.

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for Clean, Just and Competitive Transition
commitment_text: I want you to strengthen and speed up enforcement of competition rules, in close cooperation with the national competition authorities in the Member States. You will focus action on the most distortive aids and practices, notably anticompetitive agreements and abuse of dominant position. You will work to accelerate authorisation of compatible aids and transactions in strategic fields, and to ensure timely clarity for economic operators that cooperate in such sectors, for example. when pooling data for artificial intelligence experimentation. • You will work with other Members of College to vigorously enforce the Foreign Subsidies Regulation, including by proactively mapping the most problematic practices that could lead to competition distortions. • You should also actively contribute to stronger global cooperation among competition authorities. • More broadly, I expect you to work with closely with other Commissioners. Exchanging information and market knowledge in a given sector can help to ensure that new legislation contributes to fair and open competition and that competition policy is evidence-based and takes into account sectoral policies. You will work with relevant Commissioners to tackle anticompetitive practices, such as those which affect the competitiveness and sustainability of the food and farming sector. • You will address the challenges and dynamics of digital markets, including platform economies and data-driven business models. You will ensure the Commission takes rapid and effective enforcement actions under the Digital Markets Act. • You will propose to revise State aid rules to enable housing support measures, notably for energy efficiency and social housing. You will work under my guidance on all of the above issues. The Directorate-General for Competition will support you in your work on the competition portfolio. Way forward The actions listed in this letter will shape your work. However, we will need to complete and adapt as we go along and I count on your ideas and expertise in this regard. In addition to College meetings and our continuous discussions, I will invite you every six months for a structured reporting meeting to discuss progress and challenges in delivering on your mission. Reflecting the evolving nature of political priorities, the Commission must remain agile in allocating staff where most needed. I expect you to play a collegial role in this, by working with your services to identify efficiencies and contributing to corporate redeployment efforts across the Commission.

commitment_text: I would like you to visit Member States regularly. I encourage you to go local, visit places and meet Europeans from across our Union, including in cities, rural and sparsely populated areas. Our network of Representations will support you in this. You should meet with representatives from regional or local areas, notably relying on our network of EU Local Councillors, and to give more visibility to EU projects making a difference to people’s lives on the ground. When engaging with our international partners you will also be supported by our network of EU delegations around the world. I expect you to actively communicate on the Commission’s actions and decisions and explain the benefits and opportunities stemming from our work. You should also help tackle disinformation, notably by providing clear and accurate information at all times. Third, this Commission will start a new era of dialogue with citizens and stakeholders. You should organise a first edition of the annual Youth Policy Dialogues within the first 100 days so that young people can be heard and can help shape your work. This will be part of our work in embedding citizen participation in our work. We will build on the Conference on the Future of Europe to instil a true and lasting culture of participative democracy. We will choose policy areas and proposals where recommendations from a European Citizens’ Panel would have the greatest value and follow up on their proposals.

commitment_text: You will ensure that the rules on transparency and meetings with interest representatives are fully respected, including for your staff and services. We will strengthen our transparency system by extending the requirements of the Transparency Register to all managers. Fifth, we will ensure that the Commission becomes more representative of the people we serve. We have made a lot of progress in terms of gender balance across management but there is clearly more to be done. Reflecting the EU’s strength in diversity, your Cabinet should ensure gender and geographical balance. While the Berlaymont will remain your primary headquarters, you will also have an office within your Directorate-General and be expected to be present on a regular basis to work closely with your staff and services. We will collectively ensure that the Commission is an inclusive, respectful and safe workplace. Making Europe simpler and faster The success of this new Commission will be measured against our ability to meet the targets and objectives we set, notably as part of the European Green Deal. You will be responsible for the delivery of the policy objectives and targets within your portfolio. To achieve this, you should make full use of all instruments for implementation and enforcement, including infringement proceedings. You will ensure that existing rules are fit-for-purpose and focus on reducing administrative burdens and simplifying legislation. You must contribute to reducing reporting obligations by at least 25% – and for SMEs at least 35%. You should leverage the power of digital tools to deliver better and faster solutions. We must listen to all companies and stakeholders who work on a daily basis to comply with EU legislation. You will organise at least two Implementation Dialogues per year with stakeholders to align implementation with realities on the ground. You will prepare an Annual Progress Report on Enforcement and Implementation for your respective Parliamentary committees and Council formations. You will also work to stress test the EU acquis and table proposals to eliminate any overlaps and contradictions and be fully digitally compatible, while maintaining high standards. New legislation must ensure that our rules are simpler, more accessible to citizens and more targeted. You will ensure the principles of proportionality, subsidiarity and Better Regulation are respected, including through wide consultations, impact assessments, a review by the independent Regulatory Scrutiny Board and a new SME and competitiveness check. Proposals must be evidence-based and the Joint Research Centre, our internal scientific service, can support you in that work.

commitment_text: You will also be responsible for the Industry, SME and Single Market portfolio. Europe’s prosperity and industrial strategy Europe’s goals – from security to social fairness, decarbonisation to digital, tech sovereignty to leader on the world stage – all depend on starting a new era of productivity, innovation and competitiveness. This was the message I outlined in the Political Guidelines for a new plan to ensure Europe’s prosperity and also reflects the vision laid out in the Draghi report. We have a lot to draw on that gives us a competitive edge and a head start in the race to develop the economy of tomorrow – from the Single Market to our trading power and our innovation potential. There are also many emerging challenges, from greater – sometimes unfair - competition from abroad and lower access to overseas markets, to threats to our economic security. commitment_text: you will work with the Commissioner for Economy and Productivity in this regard. Crucially we must ensure that our trade and economic security policies enables our companies to benefit from markets around the world and fair global competition and European competitiveness. Free and fair trade will continue to be essential, notably when it comes to reducing our dependencies, diversifying our supply and ensuring our sovereignty. You will help guide the work on the reform of our customs systems and on the implementation of the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism. Industry, SMEs and the Single Market • I would like you to develop, with the Commissioner for Climate, Net Zero and Clean Growth, the Clean Industrial Deal in the first 100 days of the mandate, notably with a focus on decarbonisation, clean technologies and incentivizing investment. You will help build a growth-conducive regulatory framework, supporting industry in its efforts to innovate, scale up, manufacture products and provide services. You should build on the Clean Transition Dialogues and work in partnership with all stakeholders to ensure targeted solutions for each value chain. • You will work to boost investment, create lead markets for clean tech and put in place conditions for companies to grow and compete globally. • As part of this, you will present an Industrial Decarbonisation Accelerator Act, to support European lead markets for the development, production and diffusion of clean tech in industry and speed up related planning, tendering and permitting processes, in particular for energy intensive sectors. You will also lead on the implementation of the Net Zero Industry Act. • You will develop the future European Competitiveness Fund to ensure that we invest in the innovation and technologies that will shape our economy and drive our transitions. You will work with the Executive Vice-President for a Clean, Just and Competitive Transition to ensure coherence between State Aid policy and the European Competitiveness Fund. • You will lead the work on the set up of new Important Projects of Common European Interest (IPCEIs) for the most strategic sectors and technologies. • You will also lead the work on risk-absorbing measures to maximise the impact of public resources and ensure continued implementation of the InvestEU programme, in cooperation with the European Investment Bank and other implementing partners. • You will revise the Public Procurement Directives to help ensure security of supply for certain vital technologies, products and services, while simplifying the rules and reducing administrative burden. It should enable preference for European products in public procurement for certain strategic sectors and technologies.

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for Prosperity and Industrial Strategy
commitment_text: You will develop a horizontal Single Market Strategy for a modernised and deeper Single Market that promotes the cross-border provision of services and cross-border movement of goods, including essential goods. You should ensure the existing rules are fully implemented and speed up the removal of barriers. • You should work closely with Member States through the Single Market Enforcement Taskforce and seek to reinforce it. You should consider the need for a Single Market Barriers Prevention Act to fill gaps in the existing ex ante instruments, without re-opening existing directives. • You will focus on improving access to finance, simplifying the regulatory environment, and promoting innovation for SMEs. You should explore the feasibility of a dedicated SME passport to reduce administrative burden and costs. • You will lead the work to introduce a new category of small midcaps and assess whether existing regulation unjustifiably hinders their development. • To reduce administrative burden, you will strengthen the Single Digital Gateway as a one-stop-shop for anyone who wants to work, study or do business in another EU country. You will consider how to expand it with new information areas and procedures, for instance labelling, certification or permitting. • You will lead the work on critical raw materials. You will set up a dedicated EU Critical Raw Materials Platform, notably to support joint purchasing and manage strategic stockpiles, and to implement the Critical Raw Materials Act. • You will work with the Commissioner for Environment, Water Resilience and a Competitive Circular Economy to present the Circular Economy Act, helping to create market demand for secondary materials and a single market for waste, notably in relation to critical raw materials. • You will also work with the Commissioner for Environment, Water Resilience and a Competitive Circular Economy on a new Chemicals Industry Package, to simplify REACH and provide clarity on “forever chemicals”, or PFAS, working with sustainability, competitiveness, security and safety considerations in mind. • You will contribute to the European Biotech Act, leveraging the potential that biotechnologies can bring to our economy, and to the European Strategy on Life Sciences. • You will develop a steel and metals action plan. • Following the evaluation of the Standardisation Regulation, you will seek to speed up development of systemic standards for our resilience and twin transitions. • You will ensure our intellectual property policy continues to reward innovation and creativity and step up enforcement of the current rules. • You will work to enforce the Foreign Subsidies Regulation in coordination with other Members of College.

commitment_text: I would like you to visit Member States regularly. I encourage you to go local, visit places and meet Europeans from across our Union, including in cities, rural and sparsely populated areas. Our network of Representations will support you in this. You should meet with representatives from regional or local areas, notably relying on our network of EU Local Councillors, and to give more visibility to EU projects making a difference to people’s lives on the ground. When engaging with our international partners you will also be supported by our network of EU delegations around the world. I expect you to actively communicate on the Commission’s actions and decisions and explain the benefits and opportunities stemming from our work. You should also help tackle disinformation, notably by providing clear and accurate information at all times. Third, this Commission will start a new era of dialogue with citizens and stakeholders. You should organise a first edition of the annual Youth Policy Dialogues within the first 100 days so that young people can be heard and can help shape your work. This will be part of our work in embedding citizen participation in our work. We will build on the Conference on the Future of Europe to instil a true and lasting culture of participative democracy. We will choose policy areas and proposals where recommendations from a European Citizens’ Panel would have the greatest value and follow up on their proposals. Fourth, every Member of College must show a true European commitment beyond doubt and be fully independent in their action. I expect full adherence to all applicable integrity, impartiality and ethical rules from all, starting with the Code of Conduct. commitment_text: you will also have an office within your Directorate-General and be expected to be present on a regular basis to work closely with your staff and services. We will collectively ensure that the Commission is an inclusive, respectful and safe workplace. Making Europe simpler and faster The success of this new Commission will be measured against our ability to meet the targets and objectives we set, notably as part of the European Green Deal. You will be responsible for the delivery of the policy objectives and targets within your portfolio. To achieve this, you should make full use of all instruments for implementation and enforcement, including infringement proceedings. You will ensure that existing rules are fit-for-purpose and focus on reducing administrative burdens and simplifying legislation. You must contribute to reducing reporting obligations by at least 25% – and for SMEs at least 35%. You should leverage the power of digital tools to deliver better and faster solutions. We must listen to all companies and stakeholders who work on a daily basis to comply with EU legislation. You will organise at least two Implementation Dialogues per year with stakeholders to align implementation with realities on the ground. You will prepare an Annual Progress Report on Enforcement and Implementation for your respective Parliamentary committees and Council formations. You will also work to stress test the EU acquis and table proposals to eliminate any overlaps and contradictions and be fully digitally compatible, while maintaining high standards. New legislation must ensure that our rules are simpler, more accessible to citizens and more targeted. You will ensure the principles of proportionality, subsidiarity and Better Regulation are respected, including through wide consultations, impact assessments, a review by the independent Regulatory Scrutiny Board and a new SME and competitiveness check. Proposals must be evidence-based and the Joint Research Centre, our internal scientific service, can support you in that work.

portfolio_title: High Representative / Vice-President for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy
commitment_text: You will be the High Representative for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy and Vice-President of the European Commission. You take office at a time when the world is more dangerous and unstable than it has been for generations. From Russia’s war of aggression in Ukraine and a systematic attack on the rules-based international order and our common values, to a weaponisation of all types of policies, we are in an era of geostrategic rivalries. Our foreign and security policy must be designed with this reality in mind. Europe needs to be more strategic, more assertive and more united in pursuing its strategic interests and in shaping the global system. We must strengthen Europe’s capacity to act, deepen our mutually beneficial partnerships and promote our values and interests around the world. To shape this work, I will ask you to prepare regular College debates on key foreign policy issues. In addition, I would like to entrust you with the following responsibilities in your role as Vice-President of the Commission: Strengthening Europe’s security and defence • We will work closely together to ensure that Europe stands with Ukraine for as long as it takes – economically, politically and militarily – and supports its territorial integrity. We must address short term needs and long-term reconstruction efforts, and work towards a comprehensive, just and lasting peace in accordance with the UN Charter. • I would like you to ensure that security is at the heart of our action. Europe must give itself the means to defend and protect itself and to deter potential adversaries. You will jointly coordinate the work on building a true European Defence Union. • I would like you to propose, together with the Commissioner for Defence and Space, a White Paper of the Future of European Defence in the first 100 days of the mandate. • You will also work to strengthen the EU-NATO partnership, covering all security threats, including new dangers linked to cyber, hybrid or space. • More broadly, I want you to work with the Commission to ensure that we can react flexibly to new threats, including cyber and hybrid attacks. You should lead the work a new horizontal set of restrictive measures against hybrid threats against the EU, in complementarity with existing sanctions. • This should be part of a strengthened strategic approach to sanctions, including more effective enforcement and addressing the issue of circumvention.

commitment_text: You will coordinate the work on a more strategic approach to our neighbourhood. • You will work closely with the Commissioner for Enlargement on relations with candidate countries and on the Eastern neighbourhood. • You will also coordinate with the Commissioner for the Mediterranean to develop a new Pact for the Mediterranean, reshaping our essential relationship with the region. • You will take forward a comprehensive EU Middle East Strategy with a view to the day after the war in Gaza, focusing on promoting all the steps needed for the two-state solution and strengthening partnerships with key regional stakeholders. • You will work to strengthen the partnerships with like-minded countries within the G7 and beyond, notably to bolster our transatlantic partnership and strengthen our foreign and security cooperation with the United Kingdom. • In close cooperation with relevant Members of College, you will develop where appropriate our relations across the different regions. You will ensure new impetus in our mutual partnership with Africa o ahead of the next EU-African Union Summit in 2025, working closely with the Commissioner for International Partnerships. You will work on a renewed approach to the Sahel region, given the o growing risks of insecurity and instability in the region. You will deepen our engagement with partners across the Indo-Pacific o region and develop a new Strategic EU-India Agenda. You will work to strengthen our cooperation with Latin America and the o Caribbean. portfolio_title: High Representative / Vice-President for Foreign Affairs and Security Policy
commitment_text: You will ensure that Europe engages and responds to the concerns of those partners impacted by European legislation. • To be a global leader, the Union needs to take decisions in a faster and more efficient way. When putting forward proposals, I trust that you will seek to trigger the use of the clauses enshrined in the Treaties that allow decisions in the Common Foreign and Security Policy to be taken by qualified majority voting. In your capacity as Vice-President of the Commission, you will work under my guidance on all of the above issues. You will coordinate the Commission’s presence in the Foreign Affairs Council. Way forward The actions listed in this letter will shape your work. However, we will need to complete and adapt as we go along and I count on your ideas and expertise in this regard. In addition to College meetings and our continuous discussions, I will invite you every six months for a structured reporting meeting to discuss progress and challenges in delivering on your mission. Reflecting the evolving nature of political priorities, the Commission must remain agile in allocating staff where most needed. I expect you to play a collegial role in this, by working with your services to identify efficiencies and contributing to corporate redeployment efforts across the Commission. Given the scale of the challenges and the many issues in our in-tray, we must hit the ground running on day one. I invite you to take contact with your future services to prepare your hearing and the work ahead. I look forward to working closely together for Europe. Yours sincerely, Dr. Ursula von der Leyen President of the European Commission

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for Cohesion and Reforms
commitment_text: I would like you to visit Member States regularly. I encourage you to go local, visit places and meet Europeans from across our Union, including in cities, rural and sparsely populated areas. Our network of Representations will support you in this. You should meet with representatives from regional or local areas, notably relying on our network of EU Local Councillors, and to give more visibility to EU projects making a difference to people’s lives on the ground. When engaging with our international partners you will also be supported by our network of EU delegations around the world. I expect you to actively communicate on the Commission’s actions and decisions and explain the benefits and opportunities stemming from our work. You should also help tackle disinformation, notably by providing clear and accurate information at all times. Third, this Commission will start a new era of dialogue with citizens and stakeholders. You should organise a first edition of the annual Youth Policy Dialogues within the first 100 days so that young people can be heard and can help shape your work. This will be part of our work in embedding citizen participation in our work. We will build on the Conference on the Future of Europe to instil a true and lasting culture of participative democracy. We will choose policy areas and proposals where recommendations from a European Citizens’ Panel would have the greatest value and follow up on their proposals.

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for Cohesion and Reforms
commitment_text: You will ensure that the rules on transparency and meetings with interest representatives are fully respected, including for your staff and services. We will strengthen our transparency system by extending the requirements of the Transparency Register to all managers. Fifth, we will ensure that the Commission becomes more representative of the people we serve. We have made a lot of progress in terms of gender balance across management but there is clearly more to be done. Reflecting the EU’s strength in diversity, your Cabinet should ensure gender and geographical balance. While the Berlaymont will remain your primary headquarters, you will also have an office within your Directorate-General and be expected to be present on a regular basis to work closely with your staff and services. We will collectively ensure that the Commission is an inclusive, respectful and safe workplace. Making Europe simpler and faster The success of this new Commission will be measured against our ability to meet the targets and objectives we set, notably as part of the European Green Deal. You will be responsible for the delivery of the policy objectives and targets within your portfolio. To achieve this, you should make full use of all instruments for implementation and enforcement, including infringement proceedings. You will ensure that existing rules are fit-for-purpose and focus on reducing administrative burdens and simplifying legislation. You must contribute to reducing reporting obligations by at least 25% – and for SMEs at least 35%. You should leverage the power of digital tools to deliver better and faster solutions. We must listen to all companies and stakeholders who work on a daily basis to comply with EU legislation. You will organise at least two Implementation Dialogues per year with stakeholders to align implementation with realities on the ground. You will prepare an Annual Progress Report on Enforcement and Implementation for your respective Parliamentary committees and Council formations. You will also work to stress test the EU acquis and table proposals to eliminate any overlaps and contradictions and be fully digitally compatible, while maintaining high standards. New legislation must ensure that our rules are simpler, more accessible to citizens and more targeted. You will ensure the principles of proportionality, subsidiarity and Better Regulation are respected, including through wide consultations, impact assessments, a review by the independent Regulatory Scrutiny Board and a new SME and competitiveness check. Proposals must be evidence-based and the Joint Research Centre, our internal scientific service, can support you in that work.

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for Cohesion and Reforms
commitment_text: You will also be responsible for the portfolio of cohesion policy, regional development and cities. Cohesion and reforms European cohesion is about bringing Europe closer to citizens and bring Europeans closer to each other. Strengthening economic, social and territorial cohesion in the EU supports quality jobs, drives up our productivity, innovation and competitiveness and is essential to deliver on our goals linked to the twin green and digital transitions, including those in the European Green Deal. We must tackle disparities and help to improve the quality of life of Europeans. This is needed more than ever as Europe recovers together from a pandemic, lockdowns and other crises, which risked deepening divergences between citizens, regions, territories and different parts of our economies. Your task will be to ensure that the EU continues to support long-lasting reforms and investments that directly contribute to strengthening European growth. We have started this work with NextGenerationEU, our recovery plan designed to make EU economies greener, digital and more resilient. Delivering on the agreed reforms and investments set out in Member States’ Recovery and Resilience Plans by the 2026 expenditure deadline will be a significant challenge and will require consistent efforts from all Member States and the Commission. I would like you to lead this work, together with the Commissioner for Economy and Productivity, and focus on the successful and full implementation of NextGenerationEU. I want you to pay particular attention to dialogue with stakeholders and the involvement of regional and local authorities. This will be part of your work to help make European economies and societies more sustainable, resilient and better prepared for future challenges. You will ensure that people in Europe’s regions – whether in cities, coastal communities or rural areas – have real opportunities to drive growth and productivity across Europe. portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for Cohesion and Reforms
commitment_text: You will help bring people together by guiding the work on sustainable mobility and tourism to connect different parts of Europe and underpin a thriving Single Market. You will also help ensure our fisheries sector becomes more resilient, sustainable and competitive in the global market and guide the work on boosting a competitive and sustainable blue economy. This will require investment and reforms across these sectors and many more and you will work with other Members of College and with Member States to instil this approach across our work to help strengthen European cohesion for a healthier future. Supporting all regions through a stronger, modern cohesion policy In your responsibilities linked to the cohesion, regional development and cities portfolio, you will drive forward our commitment to promote economic, social and territorial cohesion and support the development of thriving, sustainable, and inclusive regions and cities. Investment in cohesion and a just transition can transform local communities. Your task is to ensure that Europe invests in and supports Member States, regions, local authorities and people in view of achieving upward economic and social convergence across the EU and that all citizens have an effective right to stay in the place they call home. • You should work on designing a strengthened, modernised cohesion and growth policy, working in partnership with national, regional and local authorities. As part of the next EU long-term budget, cohesion policy should be aligned to wider EU priorities, be more focused, simpler and more impactful and help mobilise reforms and investments. • You will strive for all citizens to have an effective right to stay in the place they call home by supporting what a community needs: public services and private activities, education and skills, health, transport and digital connectivity. You should tackle regional disparities, providing tailor-made solutions to regional and local challenges and supporting regions to catch up. • You should also address the specific challenges facing people living in regions across Europe. In this context, you will contribute to the European Affordable Housing Plan, notably to inject liquidity into the housing market and allow Member States to double the planned cohesion policy investments in affordable housing. You will also contribute to the New European Bauhaus. • You should contribute to the Climate Adaptation Plan and the European Water Resilience Strategy, paying close attention to the specific challenges of cities and regional areas.

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for Cohesion and Reforms
commitment_text: I want you to ensure the effective implementation of the Cohesion Policy, including by using the European Regional Development Fund and the Cohesion Fund, and by supporting the implementation of the Just Transition Fund. • To harness the potential of cities as innovation, growth and competitiveness engines, you should put forward an ambitious policy agenda for cities. This agenda should provide a clear vision for the future of the cities, looking at issues such as housing, climate action, digitalisation, mobility, social inclusion and equality. • You should ensure that the specific economic and social challenges facing islands are taken into account, such as housing, transport, water and waste management and continue to address challenges facing outermost regions. • I would like you to increase the visibility of EU projects. I expect you to visit projects, raise awareness, strengthen communication and speak with people on the ground to see how we can improve implementation and better cater for their needs. • In view of facilitating the reunification of Cyprus, you should, through the Aid Programme, continue strengthening social and economic conditions of the Turkish Cypriot Community and monitor implementation of the Green Line Regulation. • I would like you to work on providing support the eastern border regions most affected by Russia’s aggression to address their specific challenges and support them in the reorientation of their local economy. • I would like you to reflect on the future of cohesion policy in a larger Union and on how to accelerate institutional convergence for candidate countries. In this context, you will also contribute to the pre-enlargement policy reviews. You will work under my guidance on all of the above issues. The Directorate-General for Regional and Urban Policy will support you in the work related to your portfolio on cohesion policy, regional development and cities. Way forward The actions listed in this letter will shape your work. However, we will need to complete and adapt as we go along and I count on your ideas and expertise in this regard. In addition to College meetings and our continuous discussions, I will invite you every six months for a structured reporting meeting to discuss progress and challenges in delivering on your mission. Reflecting the evolving nature of political priorities, the Commission must remain agile in allocating staff where most needed. I expect you to play a collegial role in this, by working

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for People, Skills and Preparedness
commitment_text: I would like you to visit Member States regularly. I encourage you to go local, visit places and meet Europeans from across our Union, including in cities, rural and sparsely populated areas. Our network of Representations will support you in this. You should meet with representatives from regional or local areas, notably relying on our network of EU Local Councillors, and to give more visibility to EU projects making a difference to people’s lives on the ground. When engaging with our international partners you will also be supported by our network of EU delegations around the world. I expect you to actively communicate on the Commission’s actions and decisions and explain the benefits and opportunities stemming from our work. You should also help tackle disinformation, notably by providing clear and accurate information at all times. Third, this Commission will start a new era of dialogue with citizens and stakeholders. You should organise a first edition of the annual Youth Policy Dialogues within the first 100 days so that young people can be heard and can help shape your work. This will be part of our work in embedding citizen participation in our work. We will build on the Conference on the Future of Europe to instil a true and lasting culture of participative democracy. We will choose policy areas and proposals where recommendations from a European Citizens’ Panel would have the greatest value and follow up on their proposals. Fourth, every Member of College must show a true European commitment beyond doubt and be fully independent in their action. I expect full adherence to all applicable

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for People, Skills and Preparedness
commitment_text: You will ensure that the rules on transparency and meetings with interest representatives are fully respected, including for your staff and services. We will strengthen our transparency system by extending the requirements of the Transparency Register to all managers. Fifth, we will ensure that the Commission becomes more representative of the people we serve. We have made a lot of progress in terms of gender balance across management but there is clearly more to be done. Reflecting the EU’s strength in diversity, your Cabinet should ensure gender and geographical balance. While the Berlaymont will remain your primary headquarters, you will also have an office within your Directorate-General and be expected to be present on a regular basis to work closely with your staff and services. We will collectively ensure that the Commission is an inclusive, respectful and safe workplace. Making Europe simpler and faster The success of this new Commission will be measured against our ability to meet the targets and objectives we set, notably as part of the European Green Deal. You will be responsible for the delivery of the policy objectives and targets within your portfolio. To achieve this, you should make full use of all instruments for implementation and enforcement, including infringement proceedings. You will ensure that existing rules are fit-for-purpose and focus on reducing administrative burdens and simplifying legislation. You must contribute to reducing reporting obligations by at least 25% – and for SMEs at least 35%. You should leverage the power of digital tools to deliver better and faster solutions. We must listen to all companies and stakeholders who work on a daily basis to comply with EU legislation. You will organise at least two Implementation Dialogues per year with stakeholders to align implementation with realities on the ground. You will prepare an Annual Progress Report on Enforcement and Implementation for your respective Parliamentary committees and Council formations. You will also work to stress test the EU acquis and table proposals to eliminate any overlaps and contradictions and be fully digitally compatible, while maintaining high standards. New legislation must ensure that our rules are simpler, more accessible to citizens and more targeted. You will ensure the principles of proportionality, subsidiarity and Better Regulation are respected, including through wide consultations, impact assessments, a review by the independent Regulatory Scrutiny Board and a new SME and competitiveness check. Proposals must be evidence-based and the Joint Research Centre, our internal scientific service, can support you in that work.

portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for People, Skills and Preparedness
commitment_text: I would like you to guide the overall work on strengthening Europe’s human capital, from skills and education to building up knowledge and experiences throughout our lives, whether through studying abroad or making the most of culture and sport. Your role will be to work with Commissioners on supporting people, strengthening our societies and our social model. Europe’s way of life is defined by the protections and opportunities of our social market economy. As our economy, industry and demography all evolve, we must ensure our society works for all. Our social model, education, skills and rights must keep pace with the speed of change, ensuring that no one is left behind. This is essential for our overall prosperity and productivity, as well as the success of the twin green and digital transitions. Working with other Commissioners, you will guide the work on the European Pillar of Social Rights and help tackle the skills and labour gaps that hold back our productivity and competitiveness, while ensuring that we strengthen Europe’s social pillar and support people where they need it the most. You will also focus on training and education and the impact and the opportunities of technology and innovation in the world of work. We must reunite our society through education, supporting young people and building on the things we have in common. This is why our continued work on building a Union of equality and break down the barriers and the discrimination that too many people encounter on a daily basis. I would like you to guide the work on this to create a fairer society and social model and ensure that Europe can draw on all of its talent and potential. As part of this, you should also guide the work on supporting young people and ensuring fairness between generations. We need to give young people a greater freedom and responsibility within our societies and democracies, notably through Erasmus+, Youth Policy Dialogues and better supporting the mental health of our children and young people. I would also like you to focus on the things that define and make up our European way of life, such as strengthening our European Sport Model or promoting our culture and creative sectors and heritage. portfolio_title: Executive Vice-President for People, Skills and Preparedness
commitment_text: I want you to steer work to reach our 2030 social headline targets. You should frame this work in a new Action Plan on the Implementation of the European Pillar of Social Rights to be presented in 2025. • You will develop a Quality Jobs Roadmap together with the social partners to ensure a just transition for all. It will support fair wages, high standards for health and safety, good working conditions, training and fair job transitions for workers and self-employed, notably by increasing collective bargaining coverage. • To support this, I want you to steer our renewed commitment to strengthening European social dialogue in a time of economic and social change. Together with European trade unions and employers, you will deliver a new Pact for European Social Dialogue in early 2025. • As part of this, you will lead the work on the first-ever EU Anti-Poverty Strategy, aimed at helping people to access the essential protections and services they need and addressing the root causes of poverty. You will also contribute to the European Affordable Housing Plan. • I would also like you to focus on the impact of digitalisation in the world of work. This should notably include an initiative on algorithmic management. You will also propose to introduce a right to disconnect. • You should work on improving Europe’s approach to occupational health and safety, ensuring healthier workplaces and mental health at work. • I would like you to work on creating a more coherent framework for addressing long-term care workforce challenges, including facilitating the recognition of skills and qualifications, supporting skilling and career progression and improving working conditions.

commitment_text: I would like you to develop an EU Teachers Agenda to help improve their working conditions, training and career prospects. As part of this, you will look into launching a European Schools Alliance to encourage better cross-border cooperation and mobility between schools across Europe. You will work under my guidance on all of the above issues. You will be supported by the Directorate-General for Education, Youth, Sport and Culture and by the Directorate-General for Employment, Social Affairs and Inclusion for your work linked to skills, social rights, jobs and education. Way forward The actions listed in this letter will shape your work. However, we will need to complete and adapt as we go along and I count on your ideas and expertise in this regard. In addition to College meetings and our continuous discussions, I will invite you every six months for a structured reporting meeting to discuss progress and challenges in delivering on your mission. Reflecting the evolving nature of political priorities, the Commission must remain agile in allocating staff where most needed. I expect you to play a collegial role in this, by working with your services to identify efficiencies and contributing to corporate redeployment efforts across the Commission. Given the scale of the challenges and the many issues in our in-tray, we must hit the ground running on day one. I invite you to take contact with your future services to prepare your hearing and the work ahead. I look forward to working closely together for Europe. Yours sincerely, Dr. Ursula von der Leyen President of the European Commission

commitment_text: You will ensure that the rules on transparency and meetings with interest representatives are fully respected, including for your staff and services. We will strengthen our transparency system by extending the requirements of the Transparency Register to all managers. Fifth, we will ensure that the Commission becomes more representative of the people we serve. We have made a lot of progress in terms of gender balance across management but there is clearly more to be done. Reflecting the EU’s strength in diversity, your Cabinet should ensure gender and geographical balance. While the Berlaymont will remain your primary headquarters, you will also have an office within your Directorate-General and be expected to be present on a regular basis to work closely with your staff and services. We will collectively ensure that the Commission is an inclusive, respectful and safe workplace. Making Europe simpler and faster The success of this new Commission will be measured against our ability to meet the targets and objectives we set, notably as part of the European Green Deal. You will be responsible for the delivery of the policy objectives and targets within your portfolio. To achieve this, you should make full use of all instruments for implementation and enforcement, including infringement proceedings. You will ensure that existing rules are fit-for-purpose and focus on reducing administrative burdens and simplifying legislation. You must contribute to reducing reporting obligations by at least 25% – and for SMEs at least 35%. You should leverage the power of digital tools to deliver better and faster solutions. We must listen to all companies and stakeholders who work on a daily basis to comply with EU legislation. You will organise at least two Implementation Dialogues per year with stakeholders to align implementation with realities on the ground. You will prepare an Annual Progress Report on Enforcement and Implementation for your respective Parliamentary committees and Council formations. You will also work to stress test the EU acquis and table proposals to eliminate any overlaps and contradictions and be fully digitally compatible, while maintaining high standards. New legislation must ensure that our rules are simpler, more accessible to citizens and more targeted. You will ensure the principles of proportionality, subsidiarity and Better Regulation are respected, including through wide consultations, impact assessments, a review by the independent Regulatory Scrutiny Board and a new SME and competitiveness check. Proposals must be evidence-based and the Joint Research Centre, our internal scientific service, can support you in that work.

commitment_text: You will also be Commissioner for Interinstitutional Relations and Transparency. Trade, customs and economic security Europe needs a new foreign economic policy. While we continue to greatly benefit from open and rules-based international trade, in today’s world geopolitics and geoeconomics go together. Free and fair trade must continue to drive our prosperity but also drive down our dependencies and help develop sustainable partnerships around the world for the benefit of consumers and businesses. You will also work on an effective and modern customs system that can help protect citizens and support a level playing field for industry. As we continue this work, we will take a more assertive stance on defending ourselves from unfair competition or security risks which affect Europe. Economic statecraft and economic security will be central priorities of this Commission. • I would like you to design and implement an EU trade policy that enables Europe to reach its goals. With this in mind, trade policy should focus on the core objectives of competitiveness, security and sustainability. • You should also continue to ensure Europe leads and improve rules-based trade, notably through a reformed and strengthened World Trade Organization and through its own network of trade agreements. • You will work to finalise trade agreements under negotiation. You will lead the work to conclude ongoing negotiations, notably with countries in Latin America and the Indo-Pacific. You will also explore the scope for opening other bilateral negotiations with emerging economies and key growth centres. • You will closely monitor the full enforcement of our trade agreements on market access and rules, notably on climate, environmental and labour standards. • You will lead on Clean Trade and Investment Partnerships to bolster our competitiveness, diversify our supply chains and boost the economies of our partners. These should provide a template for striking other bespoke partnerships, for instance on critical raw materials or digital trade and data flows with trust, as part of a comprehensive offering to partner countries. • You will work on managing and deepening our partnerships around the world: You will be the Commission’s lead on relations with the United Kingdom. o
commitment_short: also be Commissioner for Interinstitutional Relations and Transparency. Trade, customs and economic...
raw_paragraph: mission I would like to entrust you with the role of Commissioner for Trade and Economic Security. You will also be Commissioner for Interinstitutional Relations and Transparency. Trade, customs and economic security Europe needs a new foreign economic policy. While we continue to greatly benefit from open and rules-based international trade, in today’s world geopolitics and geoeconomics go together. Free and fair trade must continue to drive our prosperity but also drive down our dependencies and help develop sustainable partnerships around the world for the benefit of consumers and businesses. You will also work on an effective and modern customs system that can help protect citizens and support a level playing field for industry. As we continue this work, we will take a more assertive stance on defending ourselves from unfair competition or security risks which affect Europe. Economic statecraft and economic security will be central priorities of this Commission. • I would like you to design and implement an EU trade policy that enables Europe to reach its goals. With this in mind, trade policy should focus on the core objectives of competitiveness, security and sustainability. • You should also continue to ensure Europe leads and improve rules-based trade, notably through a reformed and strengthened World Trade Organization and through its own network of trade agreements. • You will work to finalise trade agreements under negotiation. You will lead the work to conclude ongoing negotiations, notably with countries in Latin America and the Indo-Pacific. You will also explore the scope for opening other bilateral negotiations with emerging economies and key growth centres. • You will closely monitor the full enforcement of our trade agreements on market access and rules, notably on climate, environmental and labour standards. • You will lead on Clean Trade and Investment Partnerships to bolster our competitiveness, diversify our supply chains and boost the economies of our partners. These should provide a template for striking other bespoke partnerships, for instance on critical raw materials or digital trade and data flows with trust, as part of a comprehensive offering to partner countries. • You will work on managing and deepening our partnerships around the world: You will be the Commission’s lead on relations with the United Kingdom. o

portfolio_title: Commissioner for Trade and Economic Security, Interinstitutional Relations and Transparency
commitment_text: I would like you to debrief the Parliament and Member States regularly on the state of play. You will also be responsible for relations with Switzerland and other Western partners. You will work with the United States to enhance bilateral trade and o investment ties and cooperate on issues of global concern. You will manage trade and economic relations with China, in line with our o policy on de-risking not decoupling. For this, you will engage in existing fora of dialogue with a view to ensuring balanced economic relations based on reciprocity. You will address the spillover of non-market policies and practices, market distortions and harmful overcapacities. You will use our autonomous instruments as necessary. You will make a major contribution to developing a new Strategic EU-India o Agenda and strengthen our economic links in the Indo-pacific region, as well with Latin America and the Caribbean. You will also continue work with African partners to facilitate trade and promote sustainable investments. • At the same time, you will work with the High Representative/Vice-President to engage and seek to address the concerns of international partners impacted by European legislation. • You will continue to ensure that EU businesses can compete on fair terms on an international level playing field. For this you will use all of our trade defence instruments where and when needed and reflect, together with our Member States and stakeholders, if further instruments are necessary to complete and reform our toolbox. • You will also work on an effective and modern customs system that can help protect citizens and support a level playing field for industry. You will support the conclusion of negotiations on the Customs Reform package and lead on its implementation, most notably by preparing the setting up of a new EU Customs Authority. • You will help tackle challenges with e-commerce platforms and strengthen measures to prevent non-EU-compliant products from entering the EU market. • I want you to work closely with the Member States on finalising the transition process towards a fully digital customs environment. • You will evaluate the rules of origin and autonomous tariff suspensions and quotas, which are essential for trade, security and sustainability.

portfolio_title: Commissioner for Trade and Economic Security, Interinstitutional Relations and Transparency
commitment_text: I would like you to focus on deciding how and when to deploy them. You will lead the work on a new economic security doctrine, which outlines the strategic use of our economic security tools within the EU. • Central to this work is the need for Europe to be more assertive in protecting our economy from key technology leakage and security concerns. I would therefore like you to develop economic security standards for key supply chains with our G7 and other like-minded partners as part of the economic security doctrine. • You will lead on the implementation of the European Economic Security Strategy. You will work towards the swift entry into force of the revised Foreign Direct Investment Regulation and continue to deepen our discussions with EU Member States on how to better cooperate on export controls and on how to tackle risks associated with certain outbound investments. • Based on the ongoing Risk Assessments on critical technologies, you will identify proportional and balanced mitigation measures to ensure our long-term economic security, including by adding, if and when necessary, new instruments to our toolbox. • In the context of the EU’s overall sanctions policy, you will work on the effective design and implementation of trade sanctions. • You will also further deepen our dialogues on economic security with the broadest possible range of partners, whilst continuing strong cooperation within the G7. Interinstitutional relations and transparency I would also like to entrust you with the role of Commissioner for Interinstitutional Relations and Transparency. • You will lead the Commission’s work to revise the Framework Agreement on relations between the Parliament and the Commission. This will help strengthen our joint political responsibility, strengthen our dialogue, increase the flow of information, and ensure greater transparency.
